content.full_text:
September 27, 1989




Charles Anthony Szychowski
Treasurer
Riverside County American Independent
Party Central Committee
4215 Mobley
La Sierra, CA  92505-1749

Re:  Our File No. G-89-558

Dear Mr. Szychowski:

We have received your postcard, dated September 18, 1989,
concerning the Riverside County American Independent Party Central
Committee and proposed changes to the California Elections Code.
The organization of political parties is not within the
jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission.  We
suggest you consult with the state central committee of the
American Independent Party about proposed changes in the party
structure.  Changes to the Elections Code must be accomplished by
legislation.  Accordingly, you also might wish to contact your
state senator or assemblymember regarding this subject.

Sincerely,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

La Sierra, California, United States. 16, November 1,989; Thursday.
14:00 pacific time.

California Fair Political Practices Commission General Counsel
Kathryn E. Donovan & Staff,

2, October 1,989; Monday Our United States Postal Service delivered
your letter about my requesting information about how to propose
changes to Our California Elections Code. Re: Your file No. C-89-558.

I will certainly contact Mr. Robert Presley and Mr. David Kelley
about my proposals. And I appreciate your reply. And I understand
that this isnot your jurisdiction.

And I'm not trying to get your response or make you mad. I want to
provide you with two (2) of these proposed changes strictly for
your information to verify my cause for you. I want you to know
that I'm really trying to do this because you are an important
person.

And regarding your comment that I should contact A.I.P. state central
committee, I fully agree. I believe to make these valid proposals
there should be discussed like legislatively by A.I.P. State Central
Committee and other A.I.P. County Committees for their ideas.

California Elections Code.

American Independent Party County Central Committee Div. 7 pt. 4 Article 4. .
§ 9,745. Dues

Each committee may establish annual dues not to exceed twenty-four
dollars ($24) per year for elected, exofficio and alternate
members. The committee may not remove members or alternates
for nonpayment of dues.

Recall General Provisions & Initial Steps Div. 16 Ch. 1. Article 1.
§ 27,000. Application of division

This division governs the recall of elective officers of the State
of California and of all counties, cities, school districts, county
boards of education, community college districts, special districts,
[strikethrough text] judges of courts of appeal and trial courts- , and political parties.

It does not supersede the provisions of a city charter or county
charter, relating to recall.

I am, Riverside County American Independent Party Central Committee,
Member District 1, Chairman, and Treasurer,

[signature] Mick Chuck Anthony Szychowski AA,CC,CE. 11-16-89.
Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski.

Paid for by Riverside County American Independent Party Central
Committee. 1,989. Treasurer Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski,
4,215 Mobley Avenue, La Sierra, California, United States, 9,25,05-17,49.

CS1cs. 1 of 1 page. [boxed text] PARTICIPATE IN GOVERNMENT.

La Sierra, California, United States,
21, November 1,989; Tuesday.          13:00 pacific time NOV 27  0 07 AM '89

California Fair Political Practices Commission Staff,

I want to know if there are any American Independent Party affiliates
whom have filed as having checking accounts for California Statewide
Candidacies in Our Direct Primary Election in 1,990.

I want a list . I believe you all can provide one if there are any
such candidates, committee checking accounts.

Then inside this large clasp envelope are numerous replies.

1. There are 3 replies. I hope you can direct and/or place these
on the appropriate desks of the          responsible persons.

2. There is another reply for Kathryn Donovan. Place this on her
desk or with her staff.

3. There are numerous Newspaper "clips" for your files, information,
you can read these, whatever. And the subjects deal with campaigns
of political candidates, committees, Federal Elections Commission,
California F.P.P.C., etc....


I am, and reply to,
Riverside County American Independent Party Central Committee, 1,989.
Treasurer Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski. (Also Chairman & Member Dist. 1.)

[Handwritten signature] 11-21-'89.

Paid for by
Riverside County American Independent Party Central Committee. 1,989.
Treasurer Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski,
4,215 Mobley Avenue,
La Sierra, California, United States, 9,25,05-17,49.

CS:cs.
1 of 1 page; 4 separate replies,
numerous news "clips".

reply requested.

(PARTICIPATE IN GOVERNMENT.)

sections.conclusion: The organization of political parties is not within the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission. Changes to the Elections Code must be accomplished by legislation.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks about the organization of political parties, proposed changes to the California Elections Code, and seeks information about American Independent Party affiliates with checking accounts for statewide candidacies in the 1990 primary election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction over political party organization matters and directs the requester to consult with the party's state central committee and contact state legislators regarding Elections Code changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letters and communications layered together (initial FPPC advice letter from Kathryn Donovan followed by requester's responses and additional correspondence). Poor OCR quality in later pages affects extraction confidence. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in the structured format; content extracted from letter body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asks about the organization of political parties, proposed changes to the California Elections Code, and seeks information about American Independent Party affiliates with checking accounts for statewide candidacies in the 1990 primary election.

CONCLUSION: The organization of political parties is not within the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission. Changes to the Elections Code must be accomplished by legislation.

embedding.summary: This document contains an FPPC response letter dated September 27, 1989, declining jurisdiction over a inquiry about political party organization and proposed Elections Code changes, followed by the requester's subsequent correspondence with additional inquiries and document submissions.
